content.full_text:
October 7, 1976

Tom High  
Pacific Gas & Electric Co.  
77 Beale Street  
San Francisco, CA 94106

Dear Mr. High:

This letter confirms our telephone conversation of Thursday, October 7, 1976, concerning campaign statements filed with respect to the November election. It is my understanding that PG&E has filed pre-election statements (Form 460) on both the candidate schedule and the ballot measure schedule. The statement filed on the candidate schedule reflects only overhead and other resources rendered in support of the company's political action committee, while the statement filed on the ballot measure schedule discloses corporate contributions in support of or in opposition to ballot measures.

Although you have indicated a number of reasons for filing statements on both schedules, the Commission staff doubts that this procedure represents a proper interpretation of the filing requirements imposed by Government Code Sections 84200, 84203 and 84204. Accordingly, you have agreed to submit an opinion request concerning the propriety of following such a bifurcated filing schedule. Pending disposition of that opinion request or further interim advice from the staff you may continue to file on both schedules according to your present practice.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West  
Staff Counsel  
Legal Division
